HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER SEALING PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PORTIONS THEREOF
Off Calendar Feb-26-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. Matter is not contested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF'S EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER SEALING PLAINTIFF'S OPPOSITION OR, IN THE ALTERNATIVE, PORTIONS THEREOF.

Plaintiff's sealing motion is granted.

Plaintiff inadvertently included in his summary judgment opposition some information that the City and County of San Francisco had clawed back in discovery. Plaintiff filed amended versions of the filings. The City did not oppose sealing the filings containing clawed-back information, but objected to Plaintiff's amendments, contending they were substantive and did not merely remove the clawed-back information. By companion order today, on the City's motion for summary adjudication, the court has exercised its discretion to consider Plaintiff's late, amended filings.

The court grants the motion to seal. The documents sealed are set out below. The court does not make Rule 2.550 findings because these documents have been withdrawn as a basis for adjudication. Ordinarily, the court would strike documents that are withdrawn as a basis for adjudication. Here, however, the court seals them to preserve for appellate review the City's argument that the amended documents should not have been considered.

Counsel for the moving party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling and is to include a list of documents to be sealed by exact title, date of filing, and transaction number.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION / Notice Of Motion & Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-10-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION.

The unopposed motion for summary adjudication is granted as to the causes of action for failure to provide a reasonable accommodation and failure to engage in the interactive process. Defendant provides evidence that it is entitled to judgment in its favor on both causes of action, shifting the burden to plaintiff to come forward with admissible evidence creating a triable issue of material fact. Plaintiff has not opposed the motion and has not presented evidence creating a triable issue.

Proposed order submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (301/CVA)
